items.title: Matrix Concepts advances Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 with industrial land sale
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd is strengthening the industrial ecosystem within MVV City through the sale of industrial land to Hong Kong-listed Golog Holdings Ltd, as the township positions itself as an integrated industrial and logistics hub under Malaysia Vision Valley 2.0 (MVV 2.0). The collaboration reflects the property developer's strategy to attract industrial and logistics players into MVV City as part of its long-term vision to develop a future-ready and commercially competitive industrial ecosystem. Matrix Concepts said the integration of cold chain infrastructure would strengthen MVV TechValley's competitiveness as regional supply chains evolve and businesses increasingly seek resilient and connected industrial destinations in Southeast Asia.

items.title: #NSTviral Obstructed road near Titiwangsa fire station raises alarm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A viral post showing hawker stalls blocking roads near the Titiwangsa Fire and Rescue Station has sparked concerns over potential delays in emergency response. "The fire station is way too close. If there's a fire, how are fire trucks supposed to get through if tables are blocking the road?"

items.title: After Selangor ruler's decree, Ronnie Liu revisits Rukun Negara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Selangor executive councillor Ronnie Liu took heed of Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah's decree when he visited Dataran Selangor to "deeply reflect" and read the Rukun Negara once again. The former DAP leader said he was "deeply moved" during the visit as he came to better understand the history and spirit behind the national pillars. On his revisit to the Rukun Negara, Liu said many people in the past only memorised the principles, but standing there today and truly understanding the history and spirit behind it.

items.title: Philippine Supreme Court won't stop ICC arrest of senator
items.summary: MANILA: The Philippines Supreme Court refused Wednesday to grant interim relief from arrest to a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity, a court statement said. The interim ruling clears the way for the potential arrest of Senator Ronald Dela Rosa, the fugitive chief enforcer of ex-president Rodrigo Duterte's deadly war on drugs that claimed thousands of lives. "The Supreme Court, in a vote of 9-5-1, denied the prayer for a temporary restraining order and/or status quo ante order filed by Senator Ronald "Bato" M. Dela Rosa," the court statement said.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Fahmi says Putrajaya’s expenditure realignment exercise reaches 50pc of target
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said the government’s expenditure realignment exercise has achieved about 50 per cent of its target so far. He said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim informed the Cabinet that measures implemented across ministries based on current priorities had begun to show positive results. “The prime minister also informed the Cabinet that expenditure realignment measures implemented across ministries based on current priorities had begun to show positive results without affecting service delivery or the implementation of ministry programmes.

items.title: Govt hits 50pct of spending cut target, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government's review of operating expenditure across ministries has achieved nearly 50 per cent of its target, says government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. Fahmi said Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim informed the cabinet that the expenditure rationalisation exercise had begun yielding positive results without disrupting service delivery or ministry programmes. "The prime minister said that the estimated target has now reached about 50 per cent," he said at his weekly post-cabinet media briefing today.

items.title: BN, GRS and GPS reaffirm support for unity government until end of term, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim had informed the Cabinet that Barisan Nasional (BN), Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) and Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) had reaffirmed their support for the unity government and pledged to remain with the administration until the end of the current term. Fahmi said the prime minister also expressed appreciation during today’s Cabinet meeting for the continued commitment shown by component parties in the Madani government. “He said the parties had reaffirmed their support for the unity government and their commitment to remain with the administration until the end of the current term.

items.title: Malaysia's trade surges 28.6pct to record RM336.7bil in April
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's trade expanded 28.6 per cent year-on-year in April 2026, the fastest growth since September 2022, to RM336.73 billion, driven by strong export demand across key markets and products. Exports rose 36.9 per cent to a record RM182.74 billion, marking the 10th consecutive month of expansion and surpassing the previous high of RM152.77 billion recorded in December 2023 by RM30 billion. Imports increased 20 per cent to RM153.99 billion, resulting in a trade surplus of RM28.75 billion, extending Malaysia's streak of trade surplus to 72 consecutive months since May 2020.

items.title: Indonesia central bank raises interest rates by more than expected
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's central bank raised its policy interest rates for the first time in two years at a policy review on Wednesday. Bank Indonesia (BI) hiked its benchmark seven-day reverse repurchase rate by 50 basis points to 5.25 per cent. BI also raised its overnight deposit facility and lending facility rates by the same amount, to 4.25 per cent and 6.00 per cent, respectively.

items.title: Tourist crushed to death during elephant bathing session in India [WATCH]
items.summary: KARNATAKA: A 33-year-old tourist from Chennai was killed after being caught in a fight between two elephants at the Dubare Elephant Camp in Karnataka on Sunday. According to Berita RTM in a Facebook post, the victim, identified as Jyunesh, had visited the camp to experience elephant bathing activities and enjoy the natural surroundings. The incident reportedly occurred during an elephant bathing session when two elephants suddenly began fighting before one of them fell and crushed the victim.

items.title: [UPDATED] Unity govt parties pledge support until term ends, says Fahmi
items.summary: Fahmi, who is also the government spokesman, said Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim expressed appreciation during today's cabinet meeting for the continued backing from Barisan Nasional, Gabungan Rakyat Sabah and Gabungan Parti Sarawak. Fahmi said the issue of the resignation of Pandan MP Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Setiawangsa MP Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad was not raised during the meeting. Instead, he said the cabinet was briefed on preparations for the upcoming Dewan Rakyat sitting next month, including updates that two former MPs had vacated their seats.

items.title: Brother among five charged with teen's murder
items.summary: IPOH: A man and four of his friends were charged at the magistrate's court here today with the murder of his younger brother in Tanjung Rambutan last week. Siri Sakti Vell, 22, together with K. Thirucelvan, 33, Shashipal Singh Manmuhan Singh, 36, N. Thaneshwaran, 24, and P. Ukenthiran, 35, as well as another person still at large, were accused of murdering 18-year-old S.K. The offence was allegedly committed in the vicinity of the Sri Subramaniar Temple along Jalan Perpaduan Southward, Tanjung Rambutan, between 1am and 4am on May 12.

items.title: British Land beats profit expectations as AI boom lifts demand for office space
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Real estate group British Land Company reported annual profit above market estimates on Wednesday, bolstered by robust demand for office spaces from a booming AI and technology industry. Demand from AI and technology firms like Claude creator Anthropic for office space in London has helped British Land as its retail parks approach full occupancy, leading to strong rental growth. The company stuck to its 2027 earnings forecast, even as it reported an underlying profit of £294 million (US$393.6 million) for the year ended March 31, compared with analyst expectations of £291 million, according to a company-compiled consensus.

items.title: Zara inquest: Father seen only once since 2018, court told
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Mahathir Mohd Hashim was seen only once when his daughter, Zara Qairina, was at Queen Elizabeth Hospital, the court heard. "Whenever I returned to my village, I never saw her father from 2018 until now, except when Zara Qairina was at Queen Elizabeth Hospital. Zara is happy with her mother.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim confers state honours to Johor Regent’s wife and Raja Muda
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 20 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today conferred the ‘Darjah Sultan Ibrahim Johor Yang Amat Disanjungi Pangkat Pertama Dato’ Sri Mulia Sultan Ibrahim Johor (SMIJ)’ state title upon Che’ Puan Mahkota Khaleeda Johor, wife of Johor Regent Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim. According to a post on the official Facebook page of HRH Crown Prince of Johor, His Majesty also conferred the ‘Darjah Kerabat Johor Yang Amat Dihormati Pangkat Pertama (DK I)’ upon the Raja Muda of Johor, Tunku Iskandar Tunku Ismail. Also in attendance were Tunku Temenggong of Johor, Tunku Idris Iskandar Al-Haj Sultan Ibrahim, and Tunku Abu Bakar Ibrahim Tunku Ismail.

items.title: Selangor Dredging set to unlock RM192mil potential from Hotel Maya lease
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Selangor Dredging Bhd (SDB) is unlocking the value of one of its prime hospitality assets, with a long-term leasing arrangement for Hotel Maya Kuala Lumpur that could generate up to RM192.15 million in recurring rental income over 20 years. The property developer said its wholly owned unit, SDB Properties Sdn Bhd, has entered into a lease agreement with Kin Hotel KL City Centre Sdn Bhd for Hotel Maya Kuala Lumpur, located at 138 Jalan Ampang, alongside meeting and conference facilities at Plaza 138 currently used by the hotel. Kin Hotel is wholly owned by Kin Living Pte Ltd.

items.title: Brother among five charged over teen’s murder after alleged cable theft dispute
items.summary: IPOH, May 20 — A man and four of his friends were charged with murdering his younger brother in Tanjung Rambutan last week, at the Magistrate’s Court here today. Siri Sakti Vell, 22, and his friends K. Thirucelvan, 33, Shashipal Singh Manmuhan Singh, 36, N. Thaneshwaran, 24, and P. Ukenthiran, 35, together with one other individual who remains at large, are accused of murdering S.K. They allegedly committed the act in the vicinity of the Sri Subramaniar Temple, Jalan Perpaduan Southward, Tanjung Rambutan, between 1 am and 4 am on May 12.

items.title: Mother feared abduction as boy trapped in lift in Johor Baru flats
items.summary: The boy's mother, who only wished to be known as Dappa, 30, said the ordeal began about 4pm when her son went downstairs to dispose of rubbish at Block A of the Projek Perumahan Rakyat (PPR) Kempas but failed to return home. Dappa said she searched around the flats, contacted the boy's friends and checked the playground, but could not find him. "I thought my son had been kidnapped before residents nearby contacted us saying they heard screams for help from inside the lift," she said.

items.title: 26 new LRT Kelana Jaya Line trains fully operational by 2031
items.summary: Rapid Rail LRT chief operating officer Nor Azmi Mohd Yusof, said the first of the new trains is scheduled to arrive in 2029, with the replacement programme expected to be completed by the end of 2030. "Insya-Allah, by 2031, we will be able to operate with the new trains," he said during a media briefing today. Nor Azmi said while the arrival of the new trains would provide a long-term solution, Rapid Rail is taking immediate steps to keep the existing fleet running safely and reliably.

items.title: Cries of relief and joy after five acquitted of drugs charges [WATCH]
items.summary: KLANG: The silence inside the High Court as the judge delivered her verdict was broken by cries of relief and joy today after five men were acquitted of drug trafficking and possession charges. Family members of labourers K. Venaigaraj, 34, S. Yogapretip, 33, M. Ganesan, 45, G. Sharathselvam, 31, and a 19-year-old, who had packed the public gallery, wept as the court ordered the men's release. Judge Norliza Othman ordered the men's release after ruling that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case.

items.title: Six century-old shophouses destroyed in Segamat fire
items.summary: SEGAMAT: Six century-old wooden shophouses were destroyed in a fire at Jalan Abdullah, Buloh Kasap, here this morning. The 8.15am fire caused total destruction to the upper wooden floors of the shops, which included a fruit store, a traditional medicine shop, a fertiliser store, a rubber trading shop and a restaurant. "Thank goodness someone alerted us about the fire, otherwise I do not know what would have happened to us," he said.

items.title: Malaysia records strongest trust levels yet, employers most trusted institution
items.summary: But the Edelman Trust Barometer Malaysia survey showed that growing distrust among people with differing views and backgrounds could pose a challenge to sustaining the country's trust advantage. The annual trust index measures public trust in four key institutions namely business, government, media and non-governmental organisations (NGOs). According to the report, all four institutions in Malaysia remained within "trust territory", with business recording 76 per cent trust, followed by government and NGOs at 72 per cent each, while media stood at 65 per cent.

items.title: Love and war: Supreme leader urges Iran to procreate
items.summary: PARIS: In a break from his recent warlike rhetoric, the latest message from Iran's shadowy supreme leader on Tuesday set his people a more productive mission: get out and make babies. "By earnestly pursuing the correct, necessary policy of population growth, the great Iranian nation will be able to play a major role and experience strategic leaps in the future," he posted on social media platform X. Iran has a population of around 92 million – less than half that of neighbouring Pakistan and smaller than Middle East rival Egypt – but official figures show it is still the 17th most populous nation in the world.

items.title: Malaysia still has no info on Global Sumud Flotilla activists 48 hours after Israeli abduction
items.summary: SEPANG, May 20 — The Sumud Nusantara Control Centre (SNCC) confirmed it has yet to receive any updates on participants of the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 detained by Israeli forces on Monday and Tuesday. SNCC director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim said the organisation remained in the dark over the fate of the humanitarian activists nearly 48 hours after their detention. Sani Araby said participants were believed to have been placed in a “prison ship” before being taken to Ashdod Port, although official information remained limited.

items.title: Engine oil issue forces RMAF Hornet to land at KKIA
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An engine oil system emergency forced a Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) F/A-18D Hornet fighter jet to make an emergency landing at Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA) last night. RMAF chief General Datuk Seri Muhamad Norazlan Aris said the incident occurred during a routine flight, prompting emergency procedures to be activated. There was an emergency involving the engine oil system.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim pilots speedboat, dives underwater to inspect marine conditions in Mersing
items.summary: MERSING, May 20 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today personally piloted a speedboat to inspect the waters around Mersing, reflecting His Majesty’s continued concern for marine conservation and environmental sustainability. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar’s official Facebook page, His Majesty, who places strong emphasis on beach cleanliness and the preservation of marine ecosystems, also dived underwater during the visit to observe conditions beneath the surface. Earlier in the morning, Sultan Ibrahim stopped for breakfast at the Midway Rest and Recreation (R&R) area in Dusun Panti, Kota Tinggi, where His Majesty enjoyed ‘lontong’.

items.title: Three former TM USA executives no longer with company, says TM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three former employees of TM USA Inc implicated in a United States Department of Justice (DOJ) probe are no longer with Telekom Malaysia Bhd (TM). TM said it had launched an internal investigation into the individuals before any legal action by United States authorities, which ultimately led to their dismissal. It was reported earlier that the United States had charged three senior TM employees for allegedly misappropriating more than US$20 million from the Malaysian telecommunications company, the DOJ said on Tuesday.

items.title: Kelana Jaya LRT passenger numbers nearly double to 300,000 daily
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The number of Light Rail Transit (LRT) passengers on the Kelana Jaya Line has nearly doubled since 2020, with an uptick observed since the start of the Middle East conflict, according to operator Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd. Rapid Rail LRT chief operating officer Nor Azmi Mohd Yusof said weekday ridership in April averaged 289,282 passengers a day, up from 150,889 in 2020, a 109 per cent increase over six years. He said Rapid Rail would continue enhancing its services to accommodate growing demand.

items.title: Don’t get stuck: The dates Plus expects the worst traffic for the Wesak, King’s Birthday holiday bonanza
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Plus Malaysia Berhad (Plus) expects more than 2.2 million vehicles on its highway network daily during the back-to-back holidays beginning this Friday. For the East Coast Expressway Phase 2 (LPT2), traffic flow is expected to reach up to 50,000 vehicles per day during peak periods. Plus is also optimising operations by opening all toll plaza lanes and monitoring real-time traffic through its Traffic Monitoring Centre.

items.title: Nga expands 'Simen Rahmah' scheme, cuts cement prices by up to 32pct
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming has announced an expansion of the 'Simen Rahmah' initiative, cutting cement prices by up to 32 per cent to help keep housing affordable. "Our mission is clear: people's housing must remain affordable. This synergy between the public and private sectors is a concrete step to ensure the cost of living remains manageable despite global economic uncertainties," said Nga at a press conference on the Simen Murah scheme.

items.title: Rapid Rail says Kelana Jaya Line latest disruptions linked to upgrade instability, ageing trains
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 20 — Rapid Rail Sdn Bhd, operator of the Kelana Jaya Line, today acknowledged shortcomings in its service following two train disruption incidents this month. Rapid Rail LRT chief operating officer Nor Azmi Mohd Yusof said disruptions that commuters may perceive as system failures or ageing assets were actually caused by instability during ongoing system upgrades. The Kelana Jaya Line experienced service disruptions during peak hours on May 12 and May 18 following component failures.

items.title: Capital A's growth prospects stay bright despite Iran war, says HLIB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Capital A Bhd's expansion plans and earnings growth prospects remain bright despite the ongoing Iran war. Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said following its first quarter (Q1) financial year 2026 (FY26) core earnings of RM24.8 million, Capital A has now achieved two consecutive profitable quarters post-restructuring. Meanwhile, HLIB said Asia Digital Engineering (ADE), the maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) arm of Capital A Bhd, continues to deliver strong performance, supported by resilient air travel demand.

items.title: SC tightens digital asset rules to enhance investor protection
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Securities Commission Malaysia (SC) has enhanced the guidelines on recognised markets, marking a step in the evolution of the regulatory framework for Digital Asset Exchange (DAX). This aligns Malaysia's digital asset landscape with global developments, amid increasing institutional participation in the asset class. The SC said Malaysia's digital asset market continues to record steady growth, with total trading value on regulated digital asset exchanges rising to RM17.14 billion in 2025, a 23 per cent increase from RM13.93 billion in 2024.

items.title: Johor BN says it will contest state polls solo despite pressure, ‘traitor’ labels
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 20 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) remains committed to contesting independently in the upcoming state election despite being threatened, challenged and labelled traitors, the coalition’s state information chief Md Israk Abdullah said today. Md Israk, who is also Johor Umno information chief, was responding to several Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders who had called on Johor BN to consider political cooperation ahead of the state polls. Yesterday, Johor Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) vice-chief Suhaizan Kaiat called on state BN to consider forging political cooperation with PH.

items.title: Royal institution serves as anchor for national unity, constitutional supremacy
items.summary: Selangor Royal Council member Datuk Salehuddin Saidin said although Malaysia practises a parliamentary democracy system, the royal institution still plays an important role as provided under the Federal Constitution. "In this context, the royal institution serves as a check-and-balance element to ensure the government's administration remains centred on the interests of the people, national stability and the supremacy of the Constitution," he told BH. Salehuddin said the principles of the Rukun Negara, particularly the supremacy of the Constitution and the rule of law, must be understood comprehensively to ensure national unity continues to be safeguarded.

items.title: Two fined RM4,000 each in Sepang court for spreading false Budi95 subsidy claims on TikTok
items.summary: SEPANG, May 20 — The Sepang Sessions Court today fined a personal driver and a Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM) volunteer RM4,000 each for spreading false information regarding the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) initiative on TikTok. Judge Ahmad Fuad Othman handed down the sentence against Mohd Aiman Zaini Satar, 29, describing the offence as serious, warning that it could trigger public disorder. MCMC prosecuting officer Nazrul Nizam Mohd Zameri appeared for the prosecution, while Mohd Aiman was unrepresented.

items.title: Suspect held over alleged attack attempt with parang at mosque in Batu Pahat
items.summary: The remand order against the 38-year-old suspect was granted by magistrate Nurasidah A. Rahman, effective from today until Friday. This allows police to complete investigations under Section 506 of the Penal Code, including recording the suspect's statement, conducting a verification parade and carrying out further probes. The suspect later fled the mosque.

items.title: China to work with US on slashing tariffs
items.summary: BEIJING: Beijing will work with Washington on reducing levies affecting tens of billions of dollars in goods, a commerce ministry statement said Wednesday, days after US President Donald Trump visited China. The intended tariff cuts will affect goods worth "$30 billion or more on each side", the online statement, attributed to an unnamed commerce ministry official, said. The commerce ministry also said that China would restore registrations for some US beef exporters, following their lapse last year during the height of tensions with Washington.

items.title: Bina Darulaman unit bags RM251.65mil Kedah road maintenance job
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bina Darulaman Bhd's (BDB) subsidiary BDB Infra Sdn Bhd has secured a RM251.65 million state road maintenance contract from the Kedah state government. BDB Infra has continuously secured road maintenance contracts from the Kedah state government since 2015, covering several districts statewide. Over a 10-year period since 2015, BDB Infra has continuously received road maintenance contracts from the Kedah state government, covering several districts across the state.

items.title: SNCC: No confirmation on GSF participants' whereabouts [WATCH]
items.summary: SEPANG: There is still no confirmation on whether participants of the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0, including Malaysians detained by Israeli forces yesterday, have arrived at Ashdod Port. Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC) director-general Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby said lawyers from the Centre for Human Rights Research and Advocacy (Centhra) and the Legal Centre for Arab Minority Rights in Israel (Adalah) are currently on the ground monitoring the situation. View this post on Instagram A post shared by Sumud Nusantara (@sumudnusantaraofficial) He said the lawyers are also on standby in Greece, Jordan and Egypt to receive the participants upon their release.

items.title: JPJ uncovers ‘tonto’ network helping lorries dodge enforcement ops in Klang Valley
items.summary: GOMBAK, May 20 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has detected truck operators using special stickers believed to be intended to help their vehicles evade enforcement detection during operations in the Klang Valley. Sinar Harian reported JPJ Senior Enforcement Director Datuk Muhammad Kifli Ma Hassan as saying that trucking companies are believed to have paid individuals or groups known as ‘tonto’ to monitor and relay the movements of enforcement teams. He said the ‘tonto’ groups use communication devices to track enforcement officers, allowing the trucks involved to temporarily lie low until operations end before resuming their journeys.

items.title: Baby dies while asleep in caretaker’s cradle in Seremban
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 21 — A seven-month-old baby boy died while sleeping in a cradle at a caretaker’s home in Bandar Sri Sendayan here on Tuesday, according to Sinar Harian . Seremban police chief Assistant Commissioner Azahar Abdul Rahim said police received a report on the incident at 10pm. He said the complainant informed police that they had received a call from the child’s caretaker saying the baby was unresponsive.

items.title: CAAM urges Malaysia travellers to stay updated amid peak holiday travel and global flight disruptions
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — The Civil Aviation Authority of Malaysia (CAAM) has advised travellers to stay updated during the upcoming school holidays, amid global disruptions to airline operations and flight schedules. CAAM advised consumers to keep informed and monitor the latest updates from their respective airlines before travelling, as air travel is expected to surge during the upcoming school holiday period. CAAM also reminded consumers of their rights under the Malaysian Aviation Consumer Protection Code (MACPC) in the event of flight delays, cancellations, denied boarding, schedule changes, or baggage-related issues.

items.title: Telekom Malaysia says cooperating with US DOJ over alleged misconduct by former TM USA employees
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Telekom Malaysia Bhd (TM) said it will continue to extend its full cooperation with the US Department of Justice (DOJ) concerning former employees of TM USA Inc. The telecommunications company said in a statement today that it had earlier initiated its own investigation pursuant to the suspicion of misconduct by the individuals, which led to their dismissal. TM said it upholds strict governance and compliance standards and does not tolerate any form of misconduct.

items.title: MCMC plans new telecom towers to boost internet coverage in Kelantan’s Felda Chiku 2
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, May 20 — The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has proposed the construction of new telecommunications towers in Felda Chiku 2 under the National Digital Network Plan (Jendela) to improve internet coverage and enhance service stability and quality. It said that although basic internet infrastructure is available, monitoring found several locations to be blind spots requiring improvements in both coverage and service quality. “Accordingly, MCMC has conducted telecommunications service quality tests at three locations identified by complainants to assess the actual coverage performance,” it said.

items.title: Selangor CID officer dies after falling from building in Shah Alam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — A police officer died after he was believed to have fallen from a building in Section 14 in Shah Alam early this morning. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar, confirming the incident, said the male officer was attached to the Criminal Investigation Department (CID) of the Selangor police contingent headquarters, according to report published by Berita Harian today. “The Shah Alam district police headquarters control centre received information on the incident at about 3.07am this morning.

items.title: Zayn Rayyan sibling case: High Court grants father full custody of autistic child without conditions or bond
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — The High Court here today ordered that custody of the younger sibling of autistic child, the late Zayn Rayyan Abdul Matin, be granted to the biological father without any conditions or bond. According to national news agency Bernama, Zaim Ikhwan Zahari, 31, was seen emotional and shedding tears after Judge Suriyati Hasimah Mohd Hashim allowed his appeal to set aside 14 conditions and a bond imposed by the Magistrates’ Court in Shah Alam on November 21, 2025. The RM2,000 bond and additional conditions imposed by the Magistrate for a period of three years are also set aside,” she said.

items.title: Four labourers charged with murder, dumping disabled man’s body by roadside in Kelantan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Two labourers were charged in the Magistrates’ Court in Kota Bharu, Kelantan, today with murdering a 20‑year‑old intellectually disabled man at a house in Kampung Binjai, Banggu last month. Online, the accused, Muhammad Hakim Zikrullah Mohd Zamran, 25, and Mohamed Eerman Daniel Mohamed Afrizal, 27, nodded in understanding after the charges were read before Magistrate Rais Imran Hamid. According to the charge, the two men allegedly murdered Muhammad Zakir Fikry Mohd Zainal at a house in Kampung Binjai, Banggu at about 11pm on April 13.

items.title: Police recover six-inch ‘rambo’ knife dumped in paddy field linked to Jalan Titi Gajah neck stabbing case
items.summary: Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the weapon, a six-inch “rambo” knife, was found on Monday after being led to the location by the suspect. He said the knife was discovered about 200 metres from the crime scene, dumped in a paddy field area. “For this stabbing case, I would like to inform that two days ago we successfully found the weapon used.

items.title: Kajang assistant manager loses RM163,500 after falling for Facebook stock investment scam
items.summary: NILAI, May 20 — An assistant manager at a private company lost RM163,500 after allegedly falling victim to a non-existent stock investment scheme promoted on social media in March. Nilai police chief Supt Johari Yahya said the local woman, in her early 40s, lodged a police report at 6.33pm yesterday after realising she was duped by the syndicate. He said the victim, who lives in Kajang, told police she had been dealing with an unknown individual on Facebook who offered a stock investment scheme promising lucrative returns.

items.title: Malaysia steps up Ebola preparedness after WHO declares global health emergency
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 20 — The Health Ministry (MOH) has stepped up preparedness and monitoring measures following the World Health Organisation’s (WHO) declaration of an Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus as a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) on May 17. The MOH said in a statement today that no Ebola cases have been reported in Malaysia so far, but preparedness measures are being further strengthened, taking into account the risk of imported cases through international travel following outbreaks in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda. The ministry said monitoring and screening efforts are being intensified to support public health risk assessments and enable early preventive measures.

items.title: RMAF fighter jet makes emergency landing after engine issue at KKIA
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, May 20 — A Royal Malaysian Air Force (RMAF) F/A-18D Hornet fighter jet made an emergency landing at Kota Kinabalu International Airport (KKIA) last night after reportedly experiencing a technical issue, according to Berita Harian. The aircraft is believed to have developed a problem involving its left engine before landing safely at 7.47pm. It is understood that the fighter jet remains parked at KKIA’s Terminal 2.

items.title: Malaysia, Myanmar move towards diplomatic reset after leadership change
items.summary: PHNOM PENH, May 20 — Putrajaya and Nay Pyi Taw are now working to restore ties nearly five years after Myanmar’s military coup plunged the nation into diplomatic isolation. “The two foreign ministers cordially discussed matters relating to the enhancement of relations between Myanmar and Malaysia at the bilateral level and in the context of Asean. “Asean must bridge the gap because in Cebu, there were different views on post-election Myanmar.

items.title: US charges three ex-Telekom Malaysia subsidiary executives over alleged US$20m fraud scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — The United States has charged three senior employees of Telekom Malaysia (TM) for allegedly misappropriating over US$20 million (RM79.56 million) from the Malaysian state telecommunications firm, the US Department of Justice said on Tuesday. Mohd Hafiz Lockman, Mohd Yuzaimi Yusof, and Khanh Thuong Nguyen, who were senior executives at the US subsidiary of TM, were accused of using false statements and forged records to siphon funds from the company and deceive counterparties, suppliers, auditors and supervisors in the United States on various occasions between July 2020 and February 2026, authorities said. Mohd Hafiz, Mohd Yuzaimi and Nguyen could not be immediately reached for comment.

items.title: Beaufort flood toll climbs to 258 as of 8am today
items.summary: BEAUFORT, May 20 — The number of flood victims in the Beaufort district rose this morning, with 258 people from 89 households recorded as of 8am today. The State Disaster Management Committee secretariat said in a statement that the latest figure represents an increase from 66 victims across 24 households recorded at 8pm last night. It said all the victims have been evacuated to the Selagon Permanent Evacuation Centre, which was opened at 3pm yesterday.

items.title: ‘Too little, too late’: Why experts say social media giants are failing to protect Malaysian youth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 20 — Children under 16 in Malaysia will likely not be able to make social media accounts in the near future, when the provision under the Online Safety Act (ONSA) 2025 restricting underage access to social media takes effect. Australia was the first to ban social media access for under-16s. Meta is facing 2,400 lawsuits related to social media addiction in the United States.

items.title: Mindef allocates RM630,000 to boost MAF veteran entrepreneurs under master class programme, says deputy minister
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 20 — The Defence Ministry, through the Armed Forces Ex-Servicemen Affairs Corporation (Perhebat), has allocated RM630,000 to assist 180 small traders and micro-entrepreneurs among Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) veterans in joining the MAF Veterans Entrepreneur Empowerment Programme (PUVET ATM) Master Class. Deputy Defence Minister Adly Zahari said the pilot programme focuses on boosting sales, market access, productivity and branding to strengthen the competitiveness of veteran entrepreneurs in today’s market, including the use of digital technology. He said the debut initiative is being carried out through a strategic collaboration between the ministry and the Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Ministry (KUSKOP), via Perhebat and the National Entrepreneurship Institute (INSKEN), to consolidate efforts to empower veteran entrepreneurs.

items.title: Search continues in Sabah for Kalabakan elephant with severe trunk injury, probe into mutilation ongoing
items.summary: Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said a complaint regarding the injured elephant was lodged last week, prompting search and monitoring operations by the relevant authorities on May 13-14. However, the elephant could not be found during the operation,” he said in a statement. Jafry said further action would depend on any new information, sightings, or reports from the field.

items.title: Temporary diversion on landslide-hit Gua Musang-Lojing road to reopen Thursday
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, May 20 — A temporary diversion on the Gua Musang-Lojing road, closed since December 3 last year due to a landslide, will open from Thursday (May 21). Kelantan Public Works, Infrastructure, Water, and Rural Development Committee chairman Datuk Dr Izani Husin said reopening the route is crucial to restoring transport connectivity between Kelantan, Pahang, and Perak, which has been disrupted since the landslide. “The reopening of this route is undoubtedly important in restoring the transport network and revitalising the interstate economic sectors, particularly involving agriculture, tourism, and logistics.

items.title: Malaysian Haj pilgrims urged to limit outdoor activities as heat hits 48°C in Mecca
items.summary: MECCA, May 20 — Malaysian Haj pilgrims have been advised to take care of their health and limit outdoor activities following extreme heat in Saudi Arabia, with temperatures expected to reach up to 48°Celsius. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Dr Zulkifli Hasan, said peak Haj rituals and Masyair (peak phase for Haj pilgrimage) operations will take place amid high temperatures. Zulkifli said Malaysia’s Haj medical teams are now on high alert, with a range of proactive measures in place in coordination with Saudi authorities to ensure the safety and comfort of pilgrims throughout the Haj season, especially during the Masyair operations.
